Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Text Ruling

MOTION TO TAX COSTS
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS PAUL TERRELL and RICA TSENG's MOTION TO TAX OR STRIKE COSTS is DENIED in large part. Defendant State Farm General Insurance Company's claimed cost of $86 for CourtCall fees is stricken. All other costs sought to be stricken are allowed. State Farm has shown that the discovery subpoenas it served were reasonably necessary to its defense. State Farm has withdrawn its request for recovery of the CourtCall fees. State Farm is entitled to recover costs in the amount of $5,630.95. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (L&M302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Continued To Oct-23-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, PeterRoldan of Emergent Law Firm (415.894.9284) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jenny J. Chu of the law offices of PacificLaw Partners LLP (510.841.7777) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is taken under submission as of this date by Hon. Richard B.Ulmer.

Later this same date the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. State Farm has met its initial burden by demonstrating that the homeowner's policy held by plaintiffs Paul Terrell and Rica Tseng at the time of their claim contained an exclusion for "bodily injury or property damage arising out of business pursuits of any insured or the rental or holding for rental of any part of any premises by any insured." (Metzgar Declaration, Ex. 1, Policy 21.) The plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue as to whether the policy covered the Fitzgeralds' claims against the plaintiffs. It is undisputed that the plaintiffs were renting the property to the Fitzgeralds at the time of the accident that injured the Fitzgeralds. (UMF 3-5.) The quoted exclusion unambiguously creates two separate exclusions, one for business pursuits, and another for the event the property is used as a rental, shown by the use of the word "or." Consequently, the exception for "activities which are ordinarily incident to non-business pursuits" does not apply where the rental exclusion bars coverage. Summary judgment is therefore appropriate on the breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing claims because State Farm owed no duty to defend or indemnify the plaintiffs in the suit brought by the Fitzgeralds. The Fitzgeralds' claims arose out of the rental of the subject property and therefore were excluded from coverage by the homeowner's policy. Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
